SEARCH
Package edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue

Class HeapPriorityQueue<K extends Comparable<K>,V>

java.lang.Object
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.AbstractPriorityQueue<K,V>
edu.ncsu.csc316.dsa.priority_queue.HeapPriorityQueue<K,V>
Type Parameters:
K - the type of keys (priorities) stored in the priority queue
V - the type of values that are associated with keys in the priority queue
All Implemented Interfaces:
PriorityQueue<K,V>
Direct Known Subclasses:
HeapAdaptablePriorityQueue
public class HeapPriorityQueue<K extends Comparable<K>,V> extends AbstractPriorityQueue<K,V>
A HeapPriorityQueue is an array-based min-heap implementation of the PriorityQueue abstract data type. HeapPriorityQueue ensures a O(logn) worst-case runtime for PriorityQueue.insert(K, V) and PriorityQueue.deleteMin(). HeapPriorityQueue ensures a O(1) worst-case runtime for PriorityQueue.min(), PriorityQueue.size(), and PriorityQueue.isEmpty(). The HeapPriorityQueue class is based on an implementation developed for use with the textbook: Data Structures and Algorithms in Java, Sixth Edition Michael T. Goodrich, Roberto Tamassia, and Michael H. Goldwasser John Wiley and Sons, 2014
Author:
Dr. King, Diya Patel

  • Constructor Summary

    Constructors
    Constructor
    Description
    HeapPriorityQueue()
    Constructs a new HeapPriorityQueue that compares keys (priorities) using the natural ordering of the key type
    HeapPriorityQueue(Comparator<K> comparator)
    Constructs a new HeapPriorityQueue using a custom comparator

  • Constructor Details

    • HeapPriorityQueue

      public HeapPriorityQueue(Comparator<K> comparator)
      Constructs a new HeapPriorityQueue using a custom comparator
      Parameters:
      comparator - the custom Comparator to use when comparing keys (priorities)

    • HeapPriorityQueue

      public HeapPriorityQueue()
      Constructs a new HeapPriorityQueue that compares keys (priorities) using the natural ordering of the key type

  • Method Details

    • insert

      public PriorityQueue.Entry<K,V> insert(K key, V value)
      The code for this method is based on the insert algorithm on page 367 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser. Inserts a key-value pair and retruns the entry created.
      Parameters:
      key - the key of entry
      value - the value of entry
      Returns:
      a reference to the priority queue entry that was inserted

    • min

      public PriorityQueue.Entry<K,V> min()
      The code for this method is based on the findMin algorithm on page 367 in the course textbook "Data Structures and Algorithms" by Goodrich, Tamassia, Goldwasser. returns but does not remove the entry with minimal element
      Returns:
      minimal value of the queue

    • deleteMin

      public PriorityQueue.Entry<K,V> deleteMin()
      removes the entry with the minimal key
      Returns:
      smallest the entry that was deleted

    • size

      public int size()
      returns the size of the list
      Returns:
      size the size of the list
